title: OpenCloning: an Open Source web application for DNA engineering that supports automation and integration with research software
author: Lera-Ramirez, Manuel
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
OpenCloning is an Open-Source web application to plan and document DNA engineering. It allows researchers to design a DNA engineering workflow through an intuitive web interface or programmatically, and provides a standardised data model to represent DNA engineering workflows. Researchers can:  
 
  Import plasmid sequences from repositories like AddGene and genome sequences from NCBI using gene names or genome coordinates.  
  Plan cloning and design primers using common techniques (Gibson, golden gate, Gateway, etc.).  
  Plan strain and cell line engineering via CRISPR and homologous recombination, which is not supported by commercial software.  
  Automate repetitive cloning and primer design using templates, web forms or scripts.  
  Archive the entire DNA engineering workflow as a json file.    
 
  Unlike commercial alternatives, OpenCloning uses the first ever Open Data Model to represent DNA engineering workflows, and supports integration with research software. For instance, we already have a working integration with an Electronic Lab Notebook (eLabFTW).  You can access OpenCloning for free online or run your own instance locally.   Try it at    https://opencloning.org/
steps_raw:
OpenCloning is an Open-Source web application to plan and document DNA engineering. It allows researchers to design a DNA engineering workflow through an intuitive web interface or programmatically, and provides a standardised data model to represent DNA engineering workflows. Researchers can:  
 
  Import plasmid sequences from repositories like AddGene and genome sequences from NCBI using gene names or genome coordinates.  
  Plan cloning and design primers using common techniques (Gibson, golden gate, Gateway, etc.).  
  Plan strain and cell line engineering via CRISPR and homologous recombination, which is not supported by commercial software.  
  Automate repetitive cloning and primer design using templates, web forms or scripts.  
  Archive the entire DNA engineering workflow as a json file.    
 
  Unlike commercial alternatives, OpenCloning uses the first ever Open Data Model to represent DNA engineering workflows, and supports integration with research software. For instance, we already have a working integration with an Electronic Lab Notebook (eLabFTW).  You can access OpenCloning for free online or run your own instance locally.   Try it at    https://opencloning.org/
